channel.title: NYT > World > Americas
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Why Did the U.S. Allow a Russian Oil Tanker Through Its Cuba Blockade?
item.description: The ship’s arrival would bring Cuba some badly needed fuel. It also marks a shift from U.S. efforts to block oil shipments and pressure the Cuban government.
item.dc:creator: Emma Bubola and David C. Adams
item.media:description: A satellite image taken last week of the port and oil terminal at the entrance of the Bay of Matanzas in northwestern Cuba.

item.title: As Trump Squeezes Cuba, U.S. Military Exists in a Bubble
item.description: The Navy base at Guantánamo Bay sparkles at night while neighbors across a Cuban minefield struggle in darkness driven by a Trump fuel embargo.
item.dc:creator: Carol Rosenberg
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: A bowling alley at the Guantánamo Bay military base on a Saturday. Life on the base has been mostly unaffected by the energy crisis in Cuba.

item.title: Luis Carlos Rúa Fought Colombia’s Corruption in an Elephant Costume. Now He’s a Senator.
item.description: After years of anonymously documenting abandoned public works projects in Colombia, Luis Carlos Rúa revealed himself days before his election.
item.dc:creator: Genevieve Glatsky and Esteban Vanegas
item.media:credit: Esteban Vanegas for The New York Times
item.media:description: Luis Carlos Rúa, in his white elephant character costume, at an abandoned government construction site in Pereira, Colombia.

item.title: U.S. Allows Russian Oil Tanker to Reach Cuba, Despite Blockade
item.description: The tanker full of crude oil could reach its expected destination by Monday, providing a lifeline to the island amid intense U.S. pressure.
item.dc:creator: Jack Nicas and Eric Schmitt
item.media:description: Havana during a blackout this month.

item.title: Can Canada’s Left Regroup? A New Leader Will Try.
item.description: Seeking a comeback after voters flocked to Mark Carney’s Liberals, the New Democrats picked Avi Lewis, a scion of a leftist political dynasty.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai
item.media:description: Avi Lewis, center, after his election on Sunday as the leader of Canada’s New Democratic Party at the party convention in Winnipeg, Canada.

item.title: The Air Canada Crash: Before and After the Frantic Call to ‘Stop, Stop, Stop’
item.description: How a cascade of seemingly minor events led to the worst runway disaster at LaGuardia Airport in decades.
item.dc:creator: John Leland, Pranav Baskar, Olivia Bensimon, Ashley Southall, Corey Kilgannon and Jan Ransom
item.category: Port Authority of New York and New Jersey

item.title: Winter’s Gloomy Spirit Lifts as Baseball’s Blue Jays Land
item.description: In Canada, mercurial weather in March and April is known as false spring, but Blue Jays’ opening day delivered spiritual spring on a winter cold night. We’ll take it.
item.dc:creator: Shawna Richer
item.media:credit: Frank Gunn/The Canadian Press, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Andres Giménez, right, celebrates his walk-off single with Vladimir Guerrero Jr. on Friday in Toronto.

item.title: Castro Heirs Emerge Across Cuba’s Political Scene Amid Energy Crisis and Trump Threats
item.description: As Trump officials demand changes, Castro family members are suddenly popping up across Cuba’s political scene. Some even ask: Could one be the “Cuban Delcy?”
item.dc:creator: Simon Romero and David C. Adams
item.media:credit: Mauricio Lima for The New York Times
item.media:description: Honoring Fidel Castro in Santiago de Cuba after his death in 2016.

item.title: Halfway Through Lent, a Small Quebec Island Celebrates With Masks and Jigs
item.description: Few islanders still observe Lent, but they cling to a tradition once seen as defying the all-powerful Roman Catholic Church.
item.dc:creator: Norimitsu Onishi and Renaud Philippe

item.title: U.S. May Label Brazilian Gangs as Terror Groups, After Push by the Bolsonaros
item.description: The Trump administration is weighing declaring Brazil’s two largest drug gangs terrorist groups, after lobbying by the sons of jailed former President Jair Bolsonaro.
item.dc:creator: Ana Ionova and Edward Wong
item.media:credit: Dado Galdieri for The New York Times
item.media:description: Drug traffickers, part of an organized criminal group, gathered after a police operation in a favela in 2019.

item.title: Air Canada C.E.O. Apologizes for English-Language Condolences After Plane Crash
item.description: Michael Rousseau said he was “deeply saddened” that his ​inability to speak French had diverted attention from the families’ grief.
item.dc:creator: Adeel Hassan
item.media:credit: Nick Lachance/Toronto Star, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Michael Rousseau, an Air Canada veteran, took over as chief executive of the airline in 2021.

item.title: Under Carney, Canada Finally Hits NATO’s 2% Spending Target
item.description: Trump and other American presidents have criticized Canada for failing to meet the alliance’s military spending minimum of 2 percent of gross domestic product.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen
item.media:credit: Renaud Philippe for The New York Times
item.media:description: Members of the Canadian military during an exercise last month on Victoria Island in the country’s Arctic region.

item.title: ‘Public Charge’ Review: The World Is Messy. Being a Force for Good Is, Too.
item.description: A new play at the Public Theater written by Michael J. Chepiga and the former ambassador Julissa Reynoso is a diplomatic memoir of sorts, and a meditation on loving one’s country.
item.dc:creator: Laura Collins-Hughes

item.title: U.S. Military Kills 4 People in Boat Strike in Caribbean
item.description: At least 163 people have been killed in the Trump administration’s campaign against suspected drug smuggling.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: US Strikes on Alleged Drug Traffickers (2025- )
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: The Pentagon said it blew up a boat in the Caribbean on Wednesday, the 47th such attack since the campaign started in early September.

item.title: Cuban Patients Are Dying Because of U.S. Blockade, Doctors Say
item.description: Cuban health care was once the pride of the island. Now the U.S. oil blockade is upending even basic medical care.
item.dc:creator: Ed Augustin, Jack Nicas and Jorge Luis Baños
item.media:credit: Jorge Luis Baños for The New York Times

item.title: Canada’s Supreme Court Hears Case on Ability to Suspend Constitutional Rights
item.description: Quebec’s ban on religious symbols — and a measure that suspends constitutional rights — are being tested in a case with far-reaching repercussions.
item.dc:creator: Norimitsu Onishi
item.media:description: The Supreme Court of Canada in Ottawa has devoted four days this week to hearings on the notwithstanding clause, which allows governments to pass laws that suspend rights in the Constitution’s Charter of Rights and Freedoms.

item.title: Air Canada C.E.O. Draws Scorn for Delivering Condolences Only in English
item.description: The lack of French in Michael Rousseau’s speech about the deadly collision at LaGuardia Airport reignited a debate over linguistic inclusivity in Canada.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon
item.media:credit: Nick Lachance/Toronto Star, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Air Canada chief executive Michael Rousseau in 2024.

item.title: Costa Rica Agrees to Take Migrants Deported by the Trump Administration
item.description: The agreement is part of President Trump’s efforts to find governments willing to accept people who have been detained in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Emiliano Rodríguez Mega
item.media:description: Then-Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem with President Rodrigo Chaves Robles of Costa Rica during a news conference last year in San José, Costa Rica.

item.title: The U.S. Military Said It Helped Bomb a Drug Camp in Ecuador. It Was a Dairy Farm.
item.description: The Times visited a village where the United States and Ecuador said they destroyed an armed group’s training camp. Residents said it was actually a dairy farm.
item.dc:creator: Luis Ferré-Sadurní, José María León Cabrera, Annie Correal, Eric Schmitt and Federico Rios
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: The remains of a dairy cattle farm in San Martín that was burned and bombed by the Ecuadorean military.

item.title: Russian Oil Shipment Puts Focus on Kremlin Spy Outpost in Cuba
item.description: Moscow may be challenging President Trump’s effort to choke Cuba’s economy. China also has suspected listening posts on the island.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Mr. Trump has spoken of using his economic leverage to mount a “friendly takeover” of Cuba and is pressing for the ouster of its president, Miguel Díaz-Canel.

item.title: Supreme Court Seems Open to Trump Request to Block Asylum Seekers at Border
item.description: A policy of turning back many asylum seekers at the border was rescinded in 2021, but the Justice Department wants the flexibility to reinstate it as a tool for border control.
item.dc:creator: Ann E. Marimow
item.media:credit: Alejandro Cegarra for The New York Times
item.media:description: Migrants approved for asylum appointments with Customs and Border Protection lined up for permission to travel to the U.S. in Tapachula, Mexico, last year.

item.title: Government Cuts Gut the Memory of Argentina’s Dirty War
item.description: Fifty years after the military dictatorship, Argentina’s government is defunding human rights groups and promoting a revisionist account of the junta’s crimes.
item.dc:creator: Emma Bubola and Sarah Pabst
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
Coups D'Etat and Attempted Coups D'Etat
Grandmothers of the Plaza de Mayo (Argentina)
Mothers of the Plaza de Mayo
item.media:credit: Sarah Pabst for The New York Times
item.media:description: The mothers of the disappeared marching in Buenos Aires’ Plaza de Mayo this month.
